metadata.title: Poland EOR Services | Employer of Record Poland – Jackson & Frank
metadata.description: Poland EOR and Employer of Record Poland services. Hire employees in Poland without entity. Polish employment laws 2026, Poland payroll outsourcing, ZUS NFZ PPK compliance, work permit support. Onboard in 2–3 days. Book a demo.
keywords: hire employees in Poland without entity

faqs.title: Poland EOR FAQs
faqs.subtitle: Quick answers on cost, timeline, legal risks, and contracts for Employer of Record in Poland.

items.question: How much does Poland EOR cost?
items.answer: Poland EOR pricing typically includes a monthly management fee per employee plus salary, employer ZUS social security contributions, health insurance (NFZ via ZUS), PPK where applicable, and taxes. At Jackson & Frank we offer transparent pricing. Total cost depends on headcount, salary levels, and benefits. Book a demo for a custom quote.

items.question: How fast can I hire someone in Poland with EOR?
items.answer: With Jackson & Frank Poland EOR, you can onboard new hires in 2–3 business days once we have signed contracts and employee details. No local entity (sp. z o.o./S.A.) is required, so you avoid months of incorporation and registration.

items.question: What are the legal risks of hiring in Poland without an entity?
items.answer: When you use a compliant Poland EOR like Jackson & Frank, the EOR is the legal employer in Poland. They assume employment law (Kodeks pracy), ZUS, tax, and compliance risk. You reduce misclassification, permanent establishment, and local registration issues when working with an established provider.

items.question: What type of employment contract does Poland EOR use?
items.answer: Employees are hired under a Polish employment contract (umowa o pracę) compliant with the Labour Code, including probation (okres próbny), notice periods (wypowiedzenie), vacation (urlop wypoczynkowy), and mandatory ZUS and health contributions. Jackson & Frank issues the contract from our Polish entity so it is fully local and enforceable.

items.question: Can you sponsor work permits for Poland EOR employees?
items.answer: Yes. We support work permits, statements of entrusting work (oświadczenie), type A permits, EU Blue Card where eligible, and temporary residence permits for work, coordinating with voivodeship offices (urząd wojewódzki) so you can hire non-EU talent through our Poland EOR.
